backlog.title: Bug Fix Implementation
backlog.description: Fix the 5 issues (3 Major, 2 Minor) found during end-to-end validation of agent-browser-pool. Issues 1 and 5 share a root cause (boolean env var normalization) and are resolved together. Issue 2 (port race) and Issue 3 (close→rebind) are more complex boot/connect path fixes. Issue 4 (bare command classification) is a trivial dispatch fix. All fixes follow TDD (implied) and AGENTS.md sandbox-safety rules.

milestones.title: Configuration & Dispatch Fixes (Issues 1, 4, 5)
milestones.description: Small, independent fixes in the config/dispatch layer. Issue 1: make _pool_config_bool accept truthy values (1/true/yes/on). Issue 5: update pool_admin_help text (same root cause as Issue 1). Issue 4: treat bare agent-browser (no command) as meta/passthrough instead of driving (which boots Chrome).

tasks.title: Fix _pool_config_bool to accept truthy boolean values (Issues 1 & 5)
tasks.description: The _pool_config_bool function (lib/pool.sh:82-84) only treats the literal string '1' as ON; every other value (true, yes, on, TRUE, Yes) is silently OFF. This contradicts README.md:218 ('set to 1/true/yes') and pool_admin_help (lib/pool.sh:4419, '1/true/yes'). The most severe impact is the cutover safety valve: AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE=true does NOT disable pooling. Fix: make _pool_config_bool accept the common truthy set (1, true, yes, on, case-insensitive), still outputting '1' for ON and '0' for OFF so all 5 consumer sites (which use == "1") automatically benefit. Update pool_admin_help text and README env var table to match.

subtasks.title: Update _pool_config_bool to accept truthy values + sync help/docs (Mode A)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/key_findings.md (Issue 1 & Issue 5) and scout-config-dispatch.md. _pool_config_bool at lib/pool.sh:82-84 only accepts '1'. All 5 consumer sites (lines 242, 1295, 1515, 3491, 4222) use == "1" and will automatically benefit since the normalizer still outputs '1' for ON. README.md:218 promises '1/true/yes'. pool_admin_help at lib/pool.sh:4419-4421 says 'if set' or '(1/true/yes)'.
2. INPUT: The three env vars (AGENT_CHROME_HEADLESS, AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE, AGENT_CHROME_ALLOW_SLOW_COPY) are read in pool_config_init (lib/pool.sh:172-174) and passed through _pool_config_bool.
3. LOGIC: Change _pool_config_bool (lib/pool.sh:82-84) to accept 1/true/yes/on (case-insensitive) as ON, outputting '1'; everything else outputs '0'. Use a case statement or [[ ]] with pattern matching for case-insensitive comparison. Update the function docstring (lines 79-81) to reflect the new accepted values. Update pool_admin_help text (lib/pool.sh:4419-4421): change HEADLESS to 'launch Chrome headless if set (1/true/yes/on)', change ALLOW_SLOW_COPY to 'permit non-btrfs (slow) copies if set (1/true/yes/on)', change DISABLE to 'disable pooling (passthrough) if set (1/true/yes/on)'. Update README.md:218-220 env var table: HEADLESS → 'set to 1/true/yes/on', ALLOW_SLOW_COPY → 'set to 1/true/yes/on to permit', DISABLE → '1/true/yes/on = per-process passthrough'. Write/update the test: source lib/pool.sh, call _pool_config_bool with each truthy value (1, true, TRUE, True, yes, YES, Yes, on, ON, On) and assert output is '1'; call with each falsy value (0, false, no, off, '', random) and assert output is '0'. This is a pure-function test — no Chrome needed.
4. OUTPUT: _pool_config_bool returns '1' for truthy inputs (1/true/yes/on, case-insensitive), '0' otherwise. All 5 consumer sites unchanged (they already check == "1"). pool_admin_help and README accurately document accepted values.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update lib/pool.sh:4419-4421 (pool_admin_help env var descriptions) and README.md:218-220 (env var table) to reflect that 1/true/yes/on are all accepted truthy values. This rides WITH the work — do not create a separate docs subtask.

tasks.title: Treat empty command as meta/passthrough in dispatch_classify (Issue 4)
tasks.description: pool_dispatch_classify (lib/pool.sh:3085-3110) returns 'driving' when no non-flag command token is present. This causes pool_wrapper_main to acquire a lane and boot Chrome for a bare agent-browser invocation (no subcommand), which upstream treats as a help/usage request. Fix: return 'meta' instead of 'driving' when cmd is empty, mirroring the --help short-circuit.

subtasks.title: Update pool_dispatch_classify to return meta for no command token
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/key_findings.md (Issue 4) and scout-config-dispatch.md. pool_dispatch_classify at lib/pool.sh:3085-3110 returns 'driving' when cmd is empty (line ~3092: 'if [[ -z "$cmd" ]]; then printf driving'). The existing test in validate.sh expects 'driving' for no-args (22 dispatch cases). pool_wrapper_main step c (line 3500-3503) execs passthrough when class == 'meta'.
2. INPUT: $@ (the agent's argv) passed to pool_dispatch_classify. The function scans left→right, skipping flags (--session, --json, -h, etc.), and sets cmd to the first non-flag token.
3. LOGIC: In pool_dispatch_classify, change the empty-cmd branch (the 'if [[ -z "$cmd" ]]' block at line ~3092) from 'printf driving' to 'printf meta'. This mirrors the --help/-h/--version short-circuit at the top of the case statement. A subcommand-less invocation is not a driving action — upstream agent-browser with no args prints help and exits 0. Update the existing test in validate.sh (or test/validate.sh self-tests) that expects 'driving' for no-args to expect 'meta'. Also update the test for '--json' (flag-only) to expect 'meta'. This is a pure-function test — no Chrome needed.
4. OUTPUT: pool_dispatch_classify returns 'meta' for bare invocations (no command token, only flags or empty $@). pool_wrapper_main will passthrough these to the real binary unchanged instead of booting Chrome.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (bare-command passthrough is the correct behavior matching upstream; the README's META list is not contradicted). The Mode B final task (P1.M4.T1) will sweep README 'How it works' if needed.

milestones.title: Concurrent Port Allocation Race Fix (Issue 2)
milestones.description: pool_find_free_port runs outside the acquire flock (deliberately, per PRD §2.19). Two concurrent acquires can both select the same free port before either writes it to the lease. The stated mitigation ('retries on EADDRINUSE') is inaccurate: pool_chrome_launch does pool_die (fatal) on instant-exit, and _pool_launch_and_verify retries with the SAME port (cannot recover from collision). Fix: (1) detect EADDRINUSE in Chrome's log and return 1 instead of pool_die, (2) add port re-pick retry logic in _pool_launch_and_verify, (3) fix the stale comment, (4) update the concurrency test.

tasks.title: Make boot path resilient to port collision
tasks.description: Modify pool_chrome_launch to detect EADDRINUSE in Chrome's log on instant-exit and return 1 (retryable) instead of pool_die (fatal). Modify _pool_launch_and_verify to re-pick a different port on failure and retry. Fix the stale 'retries on EADDRINUSE' comment. Update the concurrency test to exercise collision recovery without the 0.3s stagger.

subtasks.title: Detect EADDRINUSE in pool_chrome_launch and return 1 instead of pool_die
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/key_findings.md (Issue 2) and scout-boot-connect.md. pool_chrome_launch at lib/pool.sh:1478-1540 calls pool_die (FATAL) on instant-exit (empty pgid, lines 1530-1534). Chrome's stderr is captured to $POOL_STATE_DIR/chrome-<lane>.log. On EADDRINUSE, Chrome's log typically contains 'Address already in use', 'Cannot start http server for devtools', or 'Couldn't bind to port'. The stale comment at lib/pool.sh:1330 and 1377-1379 claims 'retries on EADDRINUSE' which is false.
2. INPUT: port, user_data_dir, lane (args to pool_chrome_launch). Chrome log at $POOL_STATE_DIR/chrome-<lane>.log (created by the setsid redirect at line 1521). POOL_CHROME_PID (set at line 1520).
3. LOGIC: In pool_chrome_launch, in the 'if [[ -z "$pgid" ]]' block (lines 1530-1534), BEFORE the existing pool_die, grep the Chrome log for EADDRINUSE-like patterns. Use: grep -qiE 'address already in use|bind.*failed|EADDRINUSE|cannot start http server|couldn.t bind' "$log_file" 2>/dev/null. If the grep matches, log a warning via _pool_log ('pool_chrome_launch: Chrome exited immediately (port $port may be in use)'), kill the bare PID (existing kill line), and return 1 (NON-FATAL, retryable) instead of pool_die. If the grep does NOT match, keep the existing pool_die (genuine misconfiguration — broken binary, bad flags). Do NOT change the success path (pgid captured, return 0). Update the function docstring (around line 1480-1510) to document the new return-1-on-EADDRINUSE behavior. Write a test: create a fake Chrome log with EADDRINUSE text, source lib/pool.sh, call pool_chrome_launch with a fake binary that exits instantly, and assert return 1 (not pool_die). Use timeout + isolated temp HOME per AGENTS.md. Mock: use a fake Chrome binary (a script that exits 1 after writing EADDRINUSE to the log).
4. OUTPUT: pool_chrome_launch returns 0 (success), 1 (EADDRINUSE detected, retryable — NEW), or pool_die (fatal instant-exit for non-EADDRINUSE causes). The caller (_pool_launch_and_verify, S2) can now catch return 1 and re-pick a port.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update pool_chrome_launch docstring (lib/pool.sh ~1480-1510) to document the new return-1-on-EADDRINUSE behavior and the grep pattern used.

subtasks.title: Add port re-pick retry in _pool_launch_and_verify + fix stale comment
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/key_findings.md (Issue 2) and scout-boot-connect.md. _pool_launch_and_verify at lib/pool.sh:2123-2161 retries on CDP timeout with the SAME port (lines 2150-2155, $port unchanged). pool_find_free_port (lib/pool.sh:1383) excludes ports already in leases (step a, lines 1395-1400). pool_boot_lane writes the port to the lease before calling _pool_launch_and_verify (line 2227). The stale comment at lib/pool.sh:1330 and 1377-1379 says 'retries on EADDRINUSE' — false.
2. INPUT: port, ephemeral_dir, lane (args to _pool_launch_and_verify). The current port is already in the lease (written by pool_boot_lane step b). pool_chrome_launch now returns 1 on EADDRINUSE (from S1) instead of pool_die.
3. LOGIC: In _pool_launch_and_verify, add a port re-pick retry AFTER the existing same-port retry logic. Two failure paths trigger the re-pick: (a) pool_chrome_launch returns 1 (EADDRINUSE from S1) on either attempt — catch this with 'if ! pool_chrome_launch ...; then' instead of the current unguarded call; (b) both same-port CDP-timeout attempts fail (existing return 1 path). On either failure path, call pool_find_free_port to get a NEW port (it will exclude the current port since it's in the lease). If pool_find_free_port succeeds, update the lease: pool_lease_update "$lane" port "$new_port". Then retry: pool_chrome_launch "$new_port" "$ephemeral_dir" "$lane" + _pool_boot_write_chrome_ids "$lane" + pool_wait_cdp "$new_port". If the retry succeeds, return 0. If pool_find_free_port returns 1 (no free ports) or the retry fails, return 1 (caller drops lane). Limit to ONE port re-pick (do not loop). Also fix the stale comment at lib/pool.sh:1330 and 1377-1379: change 'retries on EADDRINUSE' to accurately describe the new behavior ('on launch failure, _pool_launch_and_verify re-picks a different port via pool_find_free_port and retries once'). Write a test: mock pool_chrome_launch to fail on the first port but succeed on a different port; assert _pool_launch_and_verify returns 0 and the lease has the new port. Mock: override pool_chrome_launch and pool_wait_cdp with test stubs (no real Chrome).
4. OUTPUT: _pool_launch_and_verify returns 0 (CDP ready, possibly after port re-pick) or 1 (all retries exhausted). The lease's port field is updated to the new port if a re-pick occurred. The stale comment is corrected.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update inline comments at lib/pool.sh:1330 and 1377-1379 (pool_find_free_port docstring GOTCHA) to accurately describe the new retry behavior. Update _pool_launch_and_verify docstring (lib/pool.sh ~2100-2122) to document the port re-pick retry.

subtasks.title: Update concurrency test to exercise collision recovery
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/key_findings.md (Issue 2, Testing Architecture Notes) and scout-boot-connect.md. test/concurrency.sh:264 staggers launches by 0.3s ('sleep 0.3') to avoid the TOCTOU race. The comment at line 247 explains: 'A ~0.3s stagger narrows the pool_find_free_port TOCTOU window'. The test uses _concurrency_run_one_lane per lane and asserts all lanes have distinct ports and all release cleanly.
2. INPUT: The updated _pool_launch_and_verify from P1.M2.T1.S2 (handles port re-pick on collision). The existing concurrency test structure in test/concurrency.sh (single-setup runner, N parallel subshells, _assert_all_distinct_and_nonzero).
3. LOGIC: In test/concurrency.sh, reduce or remove the 'sleep 0.3' stagger at line 264 so two concurrent acquires can collide on the same port. Update the comment at line 247 to explain that the stagger is removed because the boot path now handles port collisions via re-pick. Verify that: (a) all N lanes still succeed (none crashes with pool_die), (b) all N lanes have DISTINCT ports (assert via _assert_all_distinct_and_nonzero or similar), (c) all N lanes release cleanly. If the stagger cannot be fully removed (e.g., the test becomes flaky due to other timing issues), reduce it to a smaller value (e.g., 0.05s) that still allows collision but is less artificial. CRITICAL: this test requires real Chrome — follow AGENTS.md §1-§6 (isolated sandbox, timeout on every subprocess, single-setup runner, reap all processes). Mock: none — this is an integration test requiring real Chrome.
4. OUTPUT: Updated concurrency test that exercises port-collision recovery without the artificial 0.3s stagger. The test verifies the fix from S2 works under genuine concurrent load.
5. DOCS: none — test-only change (no user-facing/config/API surface change).

milestones.title: Close → Rebind Fix (Issue 3)
milestones.description: After agent-browser close (disconnect-only), the session lingers in the daemon's session list and Chrome stays alive. pool_daemon_connected sees both probes pass and returns 0, so pool_ensure_connected skips the rebind. The next driving command execs against a disconnected daemon binding. Fix: (1) mark the lease connected=false in pool_wrapper_main before exec'ing close, (2) make pool_ensure_connected check the connected flag and skip the early-exit when connected=false, (3) add a test for close → next driving command rebind.

tasks.title: Mark lease connected=false on close and check in ensure_connected
tasks.description: In pool_wrapper_main, after arg normalization, if the command is 'close', set pool_lease_update connected false before exec. In pool_ensure_connected, read the connected flag from the lease; if connected=false, skip the pool_daemon_connected early-exit and proceed to the reconnect path. Add a test verifying close → next driving command rebinds successfully.

subtasks.title: Set connected=false in pool_wrapper_main close path before exec
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/key_findings.md (Issue 3) and scout-boot-connect.md. pool_wrapper_main at lib/pool.sh:3479-3604 handles close like any driving command: step h (ensure_connected) → step i (normalize_close strips --all) → step j (strip_session + force_session) → step k (exec). The exec at step k (line 3604) replaces the process — nothing runs after. pool_normalize_close (lib/pool.sh:3164) is a pure argv rewriter (strips --all, sets POOL_CLOSE_ALL_SEEN). No code path sets connected=false after close. pool_lease_update (lib/pool.sh:763) takes LANE FIELD VALUE; VALUE 'false' is valid JSON (boolean).
2. INPUT: $N (the lane number, set at step e-g). POOL_CLEAN_ARGS (the cleaned argv after step j, containing the 'close' command token plus any flags like --json). The lease file at $POOL_LANES_DIR/$N.json (exists — acquired at step e-g).
3. LOGIC: In pool_wrapper_main, add a new step between step j (pool_force_session) and step k (exec). Scan POOL_CLEAN_ARGS for the 'close' command token (first non-flag token). If found, call pool_lease_update "$N" connected false. This marks the lease so the NEXT invocation's pool_ensure_connected knows the daemon binding was detached. Use a simple loop: iterate POOL_CLEAN_ARGS, skip flags (tokens starting with '-'), check if the first non-flag token is 'close'. Guard with '|| true' since pool_lease_update can pool_die on a corrupt lease (unlikely here — the lease was just read at step h, but be defensive). The lease update MUST happen before exec (exec replaces the process). Update the inline comments around step k to document this new behavior. Write a test: source lib/pool.sh, set up a temp state dir with a lease for lane 1 (connected=true), call the close-detection logic (or pool_wrapper_main with a mocked exec), and assert the lease has connected=false. Mock: override pool_wrapper_main's exec by testing the logic in isolation or by mocking $POOL_REAL_BIN to a no-op script.
4. OUTPUT: pool_wrapper_main sets connected=false in the lease before exec'ing a close command. The lease's connected field is 'false' after a close invocation. The next invocation's pool_ensure_connected (S2) will see connected=false and rebind.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update inline comments in pool_wrapper_main (around step k, lib/pool.sh ~3558-3604) to document the connected=false update on close. Add a comment explaining WHY: 'close detaches the daemon binding but the session lingers in the list; marking connected=false forces the next ensure_connected to rebind.'

subtasks.title: Check connected flag in pool_ensure_connected to skip early-exit after close
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/key_findings.md (Issue 3) and scout-boot-connect.md. pool_ensure_connected at lib/pool.sh:2306-2402 reads session, port, ephemeral_dir from the lease via jq (line 2323: 'jq -r ".session, .port, .ephemeral_dir"'). It does NOT read the connected field. The early-exit at line 2339-2341 calls pool_daemon_connected which returns 0 after close (lingering session + alive chrome). The reconnect path (lines 2344-2360) checks Chrome liveness via curl and re-binds via pool_daemon_connect.
2. INPUT: The lease JSON (now includes connected=false after close, from S1). The jq extraction at line 2323 currently extracts 3 fields (session, port, ephemeral_dir) into _f[0], _f[1], _f[2].
3. LOGIC: In pool_ensure_connected, modify the jq extraction (line 2323) to also extract the connected field: 'jq -r ".session, .port, .ephemeral_dir, .connected"'. Add 'local connected' to the locals. Set 'connected="${_f[3]:-true}"' (default to true if missing — backward compatibility with old leases). Then modify the early-exit block (lines 2339-2342): wrap the pool_daemon_connected call in a condition that checks the connected flag: 'if [[ "$connected" == "true" ]] && pool_daemon_connected "$session" "$port"; then'. If connected is 'false', skip the early-exit and fall through to the reconnect path (lines 2344+), which checks Chrome liveness via curl and re-binds via pool_daemon_connect. The reconnect path already handles the case where Chrome is alive (reconnect) or dead (relaunch). After a successful reconnect, the existing code sets connected=true (line 2348). Write a test: source lib/pool.sh, set up a temp state dir with a lease (connected=false, port pointing to a mock Chrome), mock pool_daemon_connected to return 0 (simulating lingering session), and assert pool_ensure_connected does NOT early-exit but instead calls pool_daemon_connect (mock it to return 0) and sets connected=true. Mock: override pool_daemon_connected and pool_daemon_connect with test stubs.
4. OUTPUT: pool_ensure_connected reads the connected flag. If connected=false, it skips the pool_daemon_connected early-exit and proceeds to the reconnect path (curl + pool_daemon_connect). If connected=true, behavior is unchanged (existing pool_daemon_connected check). After successful reconnect, connected is set to true.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update pool_ensure_connected docstring (lib/pool.sh ~2272-2305) to document the new connected-flag check: 'reads the connected field from the lease; if false (e.g., after a close), skips the daemon_connected early-exit and proceeds to reconnect/relaunch.'

subtasks.title: Add test for close → next driving command rebind path
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/key_findings.md (Issue 3, Testing Architecture Notes). test/release_reaper.sh:319 test_close_is_disconnect_only verifies close is disconnect-only (Chrome alive, lane persists, find_mine returns same lane). It calls pool_ensure_connected at line 373 and asserts it succeeds — but this test runs BEFORE the fix, so pool_daemon_connected returns 0 (lingering) and ensure_connected early-exits without actually re-binding. After the fix (S1+S2), this test should still pass (ensure_connected now rebinds). The NEW test should verify the REBIND explicitly. The single-setup runner pattern (_abpool_run_release_reaper_suite) is the approved pattern (AGENTS.md §4).
2. INPUT: The updated pool_wrapper_main (S1: sets connected=false on close) and pool_ensure_connected (S2: checks connected flag). The existing test infrastructure in test/release_reaper.sh (setup, _release_acquire_boot, _test_spawn_owner, assert helpers).
3. LOGIC: Add a new test function test_close_then_rebind (or test_close_marks_connected_false) in test/release_reaper.sh. Steps: (1) spawn owner, acquire+boot lane N (via _release_acquire_boot). (2) Run close: '$POOL_REAL_BIN --session abpool-$N close' (or invoke pool_wrapper_main's close path). (3) Assert the lease has connected=false (pool_lease_field $N connected == 'false'). (4) Call pool_ensure_connected $N. (5) Assert it succeeds (return 0). (6) Assert the lease now has connected=true (pool_lease_field $N connected == 'true'). (7) Assert the daemon is actually bound (pool_daemon_connected returns 0 — but now because it IS bound, not because of lingering). CRITICAL: this test requires real Chrome — follow AGENTS.md §1-§6 (isolated sandbox, timeout on every subprocess, single-setup runner, reap all processes). Add the test to the _abpool_run_release_reaper_suite function's test list. Mock: none — this is an integration test requiring real Chrome and the real agent-browser daemon.
4. OUTPUT: A test that verifies: close marks connected=false in the lease, and the next pool_ensure_connected rebinds the daemon (connected → true) instead of skipping via the lingering-session early-exit.
5. DOCS: none — test-only change (no user-facing/config/API surface change).

milestones.title: Sync Changeset-Level Documentation
milestones.description: Final cross-cutting documentation sweep. The per-file docs (Mode A) were updated in the implementing subtasks. This task sweeps README.md overview sections ('How it works', 'Safety valve') and any other changeset-level docs that span multiple issues.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Review and update README.md overview sections that span the entire changeset: 'How it works' (bare commands are now META passthrough — Issue 4), 'Safety valve' (AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE now accepts truthy values — Issue 1), and any other cross-cutting docs. This is the catch-all that prevents a coherent delta from shipping with a stale README.

subtasks.title: Update README.md overview sections for changeset-level changes
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/001_0f759fe2777c/bugfix/001_af49e87213c6/architecture/system_context.md and key_findings.md. README.md has: env var table (lines 218-220, updated in Mode A by P1.M1.T1.S1), 'How it works' section (around line 248+, lists META commands: skills, --help, --version, session list, dashboard, plugin), 'Safety valve' section (around line 236+, says AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE=1). The changeset spans Issues 1-5.
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks (P1.M1.T1.S1 through P1.M3.T1.S3). The README.md current state (env var table already updated by P1.M1.T1.S1 in Mode A).
3. LOGIC: Review README.md for cross-cutting documentation that needs updating: (1) 'How it works' section — after Issue 4, bare agent-browser (no command) is now META passthrough. The 'How it works' section lists META commands; consider adding 'bare invocation (no subcommand)' to the list or adding a note. (2) 'Safety valve' section — after Issue 1, AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE accepts 1/true/yes/on, not just 1. The section says 'AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE=1'; consider updating to 'AGENT_BROWSER_POOL_DISABLE=1 (or true/yes/on)'. (3) Check if the 'How it works' flow diagram or the 'passes through unchanged when' list needs updating for Issue 4. (4) Check if any other README section references boolean env var behavior or bare-command behavior. Update only what is stale — do not rewrite sections unnecessarily. If a section is already accurate (e.g., the env var table was updated by Mode A), skip it.
4. OUTPUT: README.md overview sections accurately reflect all changeset-level changes. No stale documentation remains.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation sync task. It depends on ALL implementing subtasks and runs last. It sweeps README.md overview/feature sections that span multiple issues. Per-file docs (env var table, help text, inline comments) were already updated in Mode A by the implementing subtasks.
